sections.paragraph:
(Aram. דְּמָא, ܕܡܐܳ), [fut. יִדְמֶה], to be like, to become like, followed by לְ, Psa 102:7 144:4 Son 2:9 7:8 אֶל Eze 31:8. With a pleonast. dat. Son 2:17, דְּמֵה־לךָ דֹודִי לִצְבִי “be thou like, my love, to a hart.” Son 8:14.

Niphal, to become like, followed by כְּ, Psa 49:13, 21 Ps4 9:21, acc. Eze 32:2. [In Thes. all the occurrences in Niphal, except the last cited, are referred to No. II.]

Piel דִּמָּה-

(1) to compare, to liken, followed by אֶל Isa 40:18, 25 Isa 40:25לְ 46:4 Son 1:9 Lam 2:13, מָה אֲרַמֶּה־לָךְ “what shall I compare to thee?” Hence to use parables, i.q. מָשׁל, מִשֵּׁל. Hos 12:11, בְּיַד הַנְּבִיאִים אֲדַמֶּה “through the prophets I have used parables” (it is better to take it thus as required by the context, than “I have destroyed,” i.e. announced destruction).

(2) to liken in one’s mind, to imagine, to think. Psa 50:21, דִּמִּיתָ הֱֹות־אֶהְיֶה כָמֹוךָ “thou thoughtest I was altogether such a one as thyself,” Est 4:13 Isa 10:7.

(3) to think, to purpose, to meditate doing something, Num 33:56 Jud. 20:5 , אֹתִי דִּמּוּ לַהֲרֹג “they thought to have slain me.” Isa 14:24 2Sa 21:5, הָאִישׁ אֲשֶׁר כִּלָּנוּ וַאֲשֶׁר דִּמָּה לָנוּ “the man who destroyed us and who meditated (evil) against us.”

(4) to remember, Psa 48:10, דִּמִּינוּ אֱלֹהִים חַםְדְּךָ “we have remembered, O God, thy loving kindness.”

Hithpael, 1 fut. אֶדַּמֶּה Isa 14:14, to make oneself like.

Derivatives דָּם No. II, דְּמוּת, דִּמְיֹון.

This signification of resemblance appears to be proper to this root; but it has another borrowed from the cognate stock דָּמָם, דּוּם namely-

II.

דמה

-

(1) to be silent, to be quiet, to rest, to cease; Jer 14:17, “my eyes are poured out in tears, day and night, and they do not cease;” Lam 3:49.

(2) causat. to make an end of any thing, hence to destroy (compare הֵדֵם, הִכְחִיד, הִשְׁבִּית, כִּלָּה No.4), especially to lay waste, to desolate, Hos 4:5, דָּמִיתִי אִמֶּךָ “I destroy thy mother,” that is, lay waste thy country; Jer 6:2, דָּמִיתִי בַת־צִיֹּון “I lay waste the daughter of Zion,” i.e. thee.

Niphal, to be cut off, to perish, used of men; Hos 10:15, בַּשַּׁהַר נִדְמֹה נִדְמָה מֶלֶךְ יִשְׂרָאֵל “to-morrow shall the king of Israel be cut off;” Isa 6:5, אֹוי־לי כִּי־נִדְמֵיתִי “alas for me! for I perish;” used of nations, Zep 1:11 Hos 4:6 of cities, countries, Isa 15:1 Jer 47:5 Hos 10:7. [See also Psa 49:13, 21 Ps4 9:21.] (In all these examples the preterite occurs, in the future the forms used are יִדַּמּוּ, תִּדַּמּוּ from syn. דָּמַם). Hence דְּמִי, דֳּמִי.
